summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of ShowMeLive virtual event platform. All requested features are working correctly: Homepage with events, Sign In flow to role selection, Viewer/Creator role options, Event detail with Stripe checkout, and Create Event page with Basic/Premium streaming packages.
passed_tests: Homepage loads with events and Sign In button
Hero section displays featured event with title, description, and Buy Tickets button
Sign In button redirects to /select-role page
Role selection page shows Viewer and Creator options with proper descriptions
Back to Home link works on role selection page
Event detail page loads with event information (title, date, time, venue, description)
Event detail shows ticket price ($45) and 'per ticket' label
Event detail shows quantity controls (+/-) that update total price correctly
Event detail shows 'Sign in to Purchase' for unauthenticated users
Event detail shows 'Secure payment powered by Stripe' note for paid events
Create Event page loads for authenticated creators
Create Event page shows all form fields (title, category, date, time, venue, price, description)
Create Event page shows Basic (FREE) streaming package option
Create Event page shows Premium ($1,000) streaming package with PRO MODE badge
Premium package selection shows warning about $1,000 payment
Submit button changes to 'Create & Pay $1,000' when Premium is selected
Submit button shows 'Create Event' when Basic (free) is selected
seed_data_creation: Created test creator user with session for authenticated testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: All three main features tested and working: 1) Pre-auth role selection at /select-role with Viewer/Creator options, 2) Stripe checkout integration on event detail page showing 'Pay $X' button for paid events, 3) Pro Mode ($1,000) streaming package on Create Event page with warning message. Note: No free events exist in database to test 'Claim Free Tickets' button, but code logic is correct in EventDetail.jsx. Backend Stripe endpoints were not tested as this was frontend-only testing.
